code:         while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):

            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions

                next(parser.stream)
                if token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                    value_token = parser.stream.current
                    if value_token.type != "string":
                        parser.fail(
                            f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a"
                            " string literal.",
                            value_token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    metadata[token.value] = parser.parse_expression().as_const()
                    continue
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            elif token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                    " assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node
note: Guessing at your dgettext/dngettext call shape (domain passed as leading Const arg, func name switches based on domain is not None) since I haven't seen your exact design -- please correct if different. My part is just the trailing metadata param + trans_metadata attribute set at the end, which is independent of the domain logic and shouldn't conflict either way. Let me know if this matches or send a counter-draft.

fields.code:
def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

My part is just the trailing metadata param + trans_metadata attribute set at the end, which is independent of the domain logic and shouldn't conflict either way. Let me know if this matches or send a counter-draft.

content:
type: AGREE
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() full method
code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if token.value == "domain" and parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                if domain is not None:
                    parser.fail(
                        "translation domain specified twice.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(domain_expr, nodes.Const)
                    and isinstance(domain_expr.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        "translation domain must be a string literal constant.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                domain = domain_expr.value
                continue
            elif (
                token.value in ("note", "created", "modified")
                and parser.stream.current.type == "assign"
            ):
                next(parser.stream)
                value_expr = parser.parse_expression()
                if not (
                    isinstance(value_expr, nodes.Const)
                    and isinstance(value_expr.value, str)
                ):
                    parser.fail(
                        f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a"
                        " string literal.",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                metadata[token.value] = value_expr.value
                continue
            elif token.value in ("note", "created", "modified"):
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                    " assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            elif parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)
                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node

code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)

                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()

                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = []
        if domain is not None:
            func_args.append(nodes.Const(domain))

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            gettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, func_args + [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            ngettext = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                func_args + [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() comma-consumption condition (single line, inside the agreed while-loop)
code:             if variables or metadata or domain is not None:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")
note: IMPORTANT correctness fix to code we already AGREE'd on. Testing our merged parse() against the task's own required example '{% trans note="test", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %}' raises TemplateSyntaxError: the old condition was 'if variables:' -- but domain/note/created/modified branches never populate the variables dict, so a comma after them is left unconsumed and the next name token fails to parse. Same bug would hit '{% trans domain="x", note="y" %}'. Fix: change the guard from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:' (one line, right after the while-loop opens). Verified this preserves the existing trimmed quirk (comma optional immediately after trimmed/notrimmed, per test_trimmed_vars) and all 830 existing tests still pass, plus the task's required example now parses/renders correctly. Please apply the identical one-line change on your side so our patches stay merge-compatible -- send AGREE to confirm.

Same bug would hit '{% trans domain="x", note="y" %}'. Fix: change the guard from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:' (one line, right after the while-loop opens). Verified this preserves the existing trimmed quirk (comma optional immediately after trimmed/notrimmed, per test_trimmed_vars) and all 830 existing tests still pass, plus the task's required example now parses/renders correctly. Please apply the identical one-line change on your side so our patches stay merge-compatible -- send AGREE to confirm.
